Scenario.title: Pikabot Ransomware Phishing Attack on Energy Sector

overview.description: The Pikabot ransomware campaign leverages phishing emails targeting employees in the energy sector. The attackers use malicious links embedded in phishing emails to gain initial access, followed by malware execution leading to data destruction and operational disruption.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Malice-driven, aiming to disrupt operations and demand ransoms

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2019-1130 [CWE-502]: Microsoft Windows SMBv2 vulnerability exploited via crafted packets to trigger remote code execution.
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 164 Name: Mobile Phishing Description: An adversary targets mobile phone users with a phishing attack for the purpose of soliciting account passwords or sensitive information from the user.
stage.description: The attackers send phishing emails containing malicious links to energy sector employees. These links redirect to compromised websites hosting malicious payloads, exploiting known vulnerabilities to establish a foothold within the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2013-0629 [CWE-502]: Adobe Acrobat Reader vulnerability exploited through malicious PDF documents to execute arbitrary code.
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 698 Name: Install Malicious Extension Description: An adversary directly installs or tricks a user into installing a malicious extension into existing trusted software, with the goal of achieving a variety of negative technical impacts.
stage.description: Upon clicking the malicious link, the victim downloads and executes a payload disguised as a legitimate file. The payload installs the Pikabot ransomware, which proceeds to encrypt critical systems and data.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 548 Name: Contaminate Resource Description: An adversary contaminates organizational information systems (including devices and networks) by causing them to handle information of a classification/sensitivity for which they have not been authorized.
stage.description: The ransomware encrypts critical infrastructure components, rendering them inaccessible. Operational technology (OT) systems are targeted, leading to potential power grid disruptions and significant financial losses due to downtime and recovery efforts.

Scenario.summary: The attack begins with a phishing email targeting energy sector employees. The email contains a malicious link that exploits a known vulnerability to install ransomware. Once installed, the ransomware disrupts operations by encrypting critical systems, leading to widespread damage and operational downtime.
